finding.message: This formatted string which depends on [library input](1) is later used in a [shell command](2).

verification.reasoning: User-controlled input `arg` is directly used in a shell command string via `'ps {}'.format(arg)` on line 352. Even though the implementation of `helpers.run_cmd` is not visible, the construction of a shell command using unsanitized user input is inherently dangerous and represents command injection (CWE-78).
answers: The exact sink is `helpers.run_cmd('ps {}'.format(arg))` on line 352. The command string passed to the shell is `'ps {}'.format(arg)`.
The only assignment to the command string is the construction on line 352: `'ps {}'.format(arg)`.
The value of `arg` derives directly from the function parameter `arg` on line 349, which is user-controlled input as indicated by the data flow from [SOURCE] line 349 to [SINK] line 352.
There are no constant-time conditions or reassignments that override the user-tainted value. The value of `arg` is used directly in the command string without any checks or modifications.

No specific defense is present. There is no sanitization, validation, or restriction on the value of `arg` before it is used in the command string. The `helpers.run_cmd` function implementation is not available, but the command construction itself is clearly vulnerable.
The user-tainted value `arg` reaches the sink directly without any sanitization or defense mechanisms.
